//: SIMULATED / PORTFOLIO -- fictional system, not a real assessment. CUI handling is hypothetical. No real DoD contract, no C3PAO assessment, no CMMC certification.

metadata.title: SIMULATED / PORTFOLIO -- System Security Plan (Excerpt): Aegis Defense Logistics Platform (NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 / CMMC Level 2)

parties.name: Ironwood Defense Systems LLC (FICTIONAL)
parties.remarks: Fictional defense subcontractor invented for this portfolio exercise. Not a real company.

parties.name: Ozirus B. Morency
parties.remarks: Portfolio author. Engineer-analyst. This is a SIMULATED artifact authored to demonstrate compliance-as-code literacy. The author holds no active clearance and no CMMC/CISSP/CISA certification, and has no production ISSO authority.

import-profile.remarks: Descriptive href to a CMMC Level 2 / NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 baseline profile. SIMULATED reference; not a fetched live profile.

system-characteristics.description: SIMULATED / PORTFOLIO. The Aegis Defense Logistics Platform is a fictional multi-tenant SaaS operated by Ironwood Defense Systems LLC that hypothetically supports defense supply-chain logistics tracking for a notional prime contractor. It is described here only to exercise OSCAL SSP modeling. CUI handling is hypothetical and simulated; no real Controlled Unclassified Information is stored, processed, or transmitted.

information-types.title: Simulated Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) -- Logistics
information-types.description: Hypothetical CUI consisting of notional defense logistics records (shipment manifests, part numbers, delivery schedules). SIMULATED data only.

status.remarks: SIMULATED. The system is a portfolio construct and is not operational.

authorization-boundary.description: SIMULATED authorization boundary. The boundary notionally includes the Aegis web application tier, an application/API tier, a managed PostgreSQL database, a centralized logging service, an identity provider for single sign-on, and administrator access paths. All cloud infrastructure is hypothetically hosted in a FedRAMP-aligned region. This boundary description exists only to model OSCAL structure and does not reflect a real deployment.

components.title: Aegis Defense Logistics Platform (This System)
components.description: SIMULATED. The overall fictional system under assessment.

components.title: Aegis Identity Provider (SSO/MFA Service)
components.description: SIMULATED single sign-on and multi-factor authentication service for the platform.

components.title: Aegis Centralized Logging Service
components.description: SIMULATED log aggregation and retention service intended to centralize audit records across tiers.

components.title: Aegis Vulnerability Scanning Service
components.description: SIMULATED authenticated and unauthenticated vulnerability scanning service for the platform attack surface.

control-implementation.description: SIMULATED control implementation narratives for 12 representative NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 requirements. Control ids use the native 800-171 numbering (for example, 3.1.1).

by-components.description: SIMULATED. Account provisioning is centralized in the Aegis Identity Provider. Only named, approved organizational users, processes, and devices are granted access; access requests route through a documented approval workflow and deprovisioning occurs on role change or separation.

SIMULATED. System access is limited to the transactions and functions authorized users are permitted to execute via role-based access control roles mapped to least privilege. Standard logistics users cannot reach administrative functions.

SIMULATED. Least privilege and separation of duties are enforced in the primary application. A legacy administrative console retains a shared elevated role pending migration to individually scoped admin accounts. Tracked in POA&M item POAM-0001.

SIMULATED. Application and identity tiers emit audit records that enable monitoring and after-the-fact investigation. The database and a batch worker tier are not yet shipping logs to the centralized logging service, leaving an audit coverage gap. Tracked in POA&M item POAM-0002.
SIMULATED. Every user and device is uniquely identified through the Aegis Identity Provider before access is granted. Shared accounts are prohibited in the primary application.

SIMULATED. Multi-factor authentication is enforced for all standard users and for administrative access to the primary application. A legacy admin console still permits single-factor password login for break-glass access. Tracked in POA&M item POAM-0001.

SIMULATED. Communications at external boundaries and key internal boundaries are monitored, controlled, and protected. TLS terminates at a managed gateway and internal service-to-service traffic is segmented by security group.

SIMULATED. The platform currently uses industry-standard TLS, but not all cryptographic modules are FIPS-validated. A rollout to FIPS 140-validated cryptographic modules for protecting simulated CUI confidentiality is planned. Tracked in POA&M item POAM-0003.
SIMULATED. System flaws are identified, reported, and corrected. Operating system and dependency patches are applied on a monthly cadence with emergency patching for critical advisories.
SIMULATED. Baseline configurations and inventories are established and maintained as infrastructure-as-code in version control, with peer-reviewed change management for the development lifecycle.

SIMULATED. Risk to operations and assets is periodically assessed. Vulnerability scanning currently runs quarterly rather than the targeted monthly cadence, reducing risk-assessment freshness. Tracked in POA&M item POAM-0005.

SIMULATED. Security awareness for managers, administrators, and users on security risks and applicable policies exists ad hoc. A formal recurring annual training cadence with completion tracking is planned. Tracked in POA&M item POAM-0004.
